---
title: "Kaifang Luo: Clean Energy Technologies Deployment"
description: "Kaifang will share her research"
image: https://deeppolicylab.github.io/files/headshots/kaifang-luo.jpeg
author: "Climate Guest Speakers"
date: "2024-09-30"
categories: [Climate Insights]
#page-layout: full
#bibliography: references.bib
#csl: chicago-syllabus.csl
#suppress-bibliography: true
#link-citations: false
#citations-hover: false
listing:  #an example of listing external links
#  template: item-default-external.ejs
- id: speaker
  sort: "date asc"
  type: grid
  fields: [image, title, subtitle]
  grid-item-align: center
  grid-columns: 2
  image-height: 300px
#  categories: true
#  sort-ui: true
#  filter-ui: true
  contents:
    - path: https://kaifangluo.com/  #linkedin profile or personal website
      image: https://deeppolicylab.github.io/files/headshots/kaifang-luo.jpeg
      title: "Kaifang Luo"
#      author: 
      subtitle: "Post-doc, CUNY Baruch College"
#      date: "2024-03-12"
#      categories: [] 
page-navigation: false
title-block-banner: false
#toc: false
---

## Title

Clean Energy Technologies Deployment

### Time

Monday, September 30, 2024, 6:00PM - 7:00PM

### Venue 
Online via zoom. Please [register](https://forms.gle/65hnhJPmnYaz5utG9) to participate.

## About

More information about the speaker's session.

## Speaker

:::{#speaker}
:::


### Bio

I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at the City University of New York, Baruch College.

I specialize in Energy Policy and Environmental Economics. A significant portion of my research has examined nonlinear electricity pricing, energy demand, renewable energy adoption (e.g., solar photovoltaics and home batteries), and local economic impacts of Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS). I obtained my Ph.D. degree in public policy (with a focus on energy economics) from the School of Public Policy University of Maryland.

My studies have been published in the Journal of Cleaner Production, and Resources, Conservation and Recycling. A number of my research papers have been presented at conferences such as NBER, AERE, APPAM, and USAEE.

## Readings



<!--Include social share buttons-->

{{< include /files/includes/_socialshare.qmd >}}
